metadata.archetype_justification: The application owns persistent state in PostgreSQL (users, websites, sessions, events, revenue) and exposes CRUD operations across 75 API route handlers managing entity lifecycle. Classified as stateful-crud.

findings.title: No managed compute — self-hosted Docker containers
findings.description: All compute runs as self-hosted Docker containers with no managed container orchestration (ECS, EKS, Fargate) or serverless (Lambda).
findings.gap: No elastic scaling, manual patching, no health-based replacement. Single container deployment cannot respond to traffic spikes.
findings.recommendation: Migrate to ECS on Fargate or EKS (preferred) for managed container orchestration. The existing Dockerfile and health check endpoint provide a strong foundation.

findings.title: Self-managed PostgreSQL in Docker container
findings.description: PostgreSQL 15 runs self-managed in a Docker container. No managed database services (RDS, Aurora, DynamoDB) are configured.
findings.gap: No automated failover, no managed backups, manual patching. A container failure results in database unavailability.
findings.recommendation: Migrate to Aurora PostgreSQL (preferred). The application already supports read replicas and uses environment-variable-based connection strings, making migration straightforward.

findings.title: No workflow orchestration for multi-step operations
findings.description: Multi-step business operations (event ingestion pipeline, report generation) are handled as inline application logic with no dedicated workflow orchestration.
findings.gap: No dedicated orchestration, error handling, or retry mechanisms for multi-step operations. Failures in the event ingestion pipeline are handled ad-hoc.
findings.recommendation: Adopt AWS Step Functions for multi-step workflows such as the event ingestion pipeline and report generation.

findings.title: Self-managed Kafka for async messaging
findings.description: Self-managed Kafka (kafkajs library) is used for async event ingestion to ClickHouse. No managed messaging services (SQS, SNS, EventBridge, MSK) are in use.
findings.gap: Kafka requires manual broker management, scaling, and patching. Cross-service state changes are handled synchronously.
findings.recommendation: Replace self-managed Kafka with Amazon MSK Serverless or EventBridge (preferred). The existing Kafka producer pattern maps directly to managed alternatives.

findings.title: No network security configuration
findings.description: No VPC, subnet, security group, or network segmentation configuration exists. Docker Compose exposes port 3000 directly with no network isolation between tiers.
findings.gap: No network security — database and application on same network with no segmentation, no private subnets, no security groups.
findings.recommendation: Deploy into a VPC with private subnets for the database tier and public subnets behind ALB for the application tier.

findings.title: No API Gateway or load balancer
findings.description: No API Gateway, ALB, or CloudFront configured. The application serves directly on port 3000 with no edge protection.
findings.gap: No throttling, no DDoS protection, no centralized auth at the network edge. Direct service exposure.
findings.recommendation: Deploy API Gateway (preferred) for throttling, auth, and request validation. Use CloudFront for global tracker script distribution.

findings.title: No auto-scaling configured
findings.description: Single instance deployment with no auto-scaling. Cannot respond to analytics collection traffic spikes.
findings.gap: Static single-instance capacity. Traffic spikes from viral content or bot attacks cannot be absorbed.
findings.recommendation: Configure auto-scaling on ECS/EKS services. The application's stateless design (sessions in Redis) makes horizontal scaling straightforward.

findings.title: No backup and recovery configuration
findings.description: PostgreSQL data stored in Docker volume with no automated backups, no PITR, no snapshot lifecycle.
findings.gap: No backup configuration — data loss from volume failure has no recovery path.
findings.recommendation: Aurora PostgreSQL provides automated backups with 35-day retention and PITR by default. If self-managed, implement pg_dump to S3.

findings.title: Single-instance deployment — no high availability
findings.description: All resources run as single instances with no multi-AZ deployment. Any host failure takes down the entire service.
findings.gap: Single-AZ, single-instance deployment. No fault isolation or automatic recovery from infrastructure failures.
findings.recommendation: Deploy across 2+ AZs using ECS/EKS with multi-AZ task placement and Aurora Multi-AZ automatic failover.

findings.title: No Infrastructure as Code
findings.description: 0% IaC coverage. No Terraform, CDK, CloudFormation, Helm, or Kustomize. Only docker-compose and PaaS configs (Heroku, Netlify) exist.
findings.gap: Cannot reproduce environments, no audit trail for infrastructure changes, no disaster recovery automation.
findings.recommendation: Adopt AWS CDK (TypeScript) to define all infrastructure. Start with compute and database layers as highest-impact targets.

findings.title: CI/CD automation lacks deployment stage
findings.description: GitHub Actions CI (build+test) and CD (Docker image publish) exist but no automated deployment stage. Images are published but not deployed.
findings.gap: No automated deployment — images published but deployment requires manual action. No IaC deployment automation.
findings.recommendation: Add deployment stage to CD pipeline targeting ECS/EKS. Implement environment promotion (dev → staging → production).

findings.title: Monolith with shared database coupling
findings.description: Single deployable monolith with identifiable modules but shared PostgreSQL database, direct cross-module table joins, and single Docker image.
findings.gap: No independent deployability. All 75 API routes deploy together. Shared database prevents independent scaling of modules.
findings.recommendation: Begin Strangler Fig decomposition starting with event collection service and analytics query service.

findings.title: Primarily synchronous communication patterns
findings.description: Main application flow is entirely synchronous. Kafka exists only for the optional ClickHouse event ingestion path. Cross-domain state changes use synchronous DB transactions.
findings.gap: State changes crossing domain boundaries are tightly coupled via synchronous transactions. No event-driven decoupling.
findings.recommendation: Introduce EventBridge for cross-domain state change events. Convert event ingestion to always use managed async messaging.

findings.title: Long-running analytics queries handled synchronously
findings.description: Analytics queries over large datasets and report generation are synchronous. No async job processing or status polling for heavy operations.
findings.gap: Analytics queries may exceed 30 seconds under load with no timeout protection or async offloading.
findings.recommendation: Implement async job processing for report generation using Step Functions or SQS + Lambda workers. Add status polling endpoints.

findings.title: No API versioning strategy
findings.description: 75 API routes use unversioned paths. No /v1/ prefixes, no version headers, no versioning annotations. Breaking changes deploy directly.
findings.gap: No versioning — critical for the tracker script embedded on third-party websites that cannot be updated atomically.
findings.recommendation: Implement URL-path versioning (/api/v1/) especially for the public data collection endpoint and tracker script.

findings.title: Environment variables for service endpoints — no dynamic discovery
findings.description: External dependencies configured via environment variables (DATABASE_URL, REDIS_URL, KAFKA_URL). No dynamic service discovery.
findings.gap: Hard-configured endpoints create deployment coupling. No dynamic resolution when decomposed into microservices.
findings.recommendation: Adopt Kubernetes service discovery or AWS Cloud Map when migrating to EKS. Acceptable for monolith phase.

findings.title: No managed object storage for unstructured data
findings.description: No S3 or managed object storage. GeoIP database files stored locally on container filesystem. No document parsing pipeline.
findings.gap: GeoIP data on local filesystem. No S3 for data exports or backup artifacts.
findings.recommendation: Store GeoIP databases in S3. Use S3 for report exports and backup artifacts with lifecycle policies.

findings.title: Mostly centralized data access with some raw SQL bypass
findings.description: Prisma ORM with 9 query modules and raw SQL analytics layer. Centralized routing in db.ts. Some direct rawQuery usage bypasses module structure.
findings.gap: rawQuery function allows arbitrary SQL from any module, partially bypassing the centralized query pattern.
findings.recommendation: Maintain query module structure during decomposition. Ensure all data access routes through the query layer.

findings.title: PostgreSQL 15 pinned but no upgrade procedure documented
findings.description: PostgreSQL 15 explicitly pinned in docker-compose. Not approaching EOL (2027) but no documented version-update procedure.
findings.gap: No documented upgrade procedure covering downtime, rollback, or risk acknowledgment.
findings.recommendation: Document upgrade procedure. Plan migration to Aurora PostgreSQL which handles major version upgrades with managed blue/green.

findings.title: No encryption at rest for database storage
findings.description: PostgreSQL data in unencrypted Docker volume. User data stored in plaintext on disk (usernames, session data, website domains).
findings.gap: No at-rest encryption for the database volume containing PII.
findings.recommendation: Migrate to Aurora PostgreSQL which provides encryption at rest by default. Use customer-managed KMS keys.

findings.title: JWT auth on management APIs; collection endpoint intentionally open
findings.description: JWT Bearer token auth with AES-256-GCM encryption on all management endpoints. Collection endpoint (/api/send) intentionally unauthenticated for third-party use.
findings.gap: Open collection endpoint has no gateway-level throttling. Internal endpoints lack additional network isolation.
findings.recommendation: Add API Gateway throttling for the collection endpoint. Network-level isolation for internal endpoints.

findings.title: Self-managed auth with SSO capability
findings.description: Application manages its own authentication (username/password in PostgreSQL). SSO support exists but is not the default authentication path.
findings.gap: Primary auth is self-managed. Not integrated with centralized IdP by default.
findings.recommendation: Integrate with Amazon Cognito as centralized IdP. The existing SSO endpoint provides the federation foundation.

findings.title: Secrets in environment variables with no rotation
findings.description: All secrets via environment variables. No plaintext in source. No Secrets Manager, no rotation, no encryption for env vars.
findings.gap: Production credentials in plain environment variables with no rotation mechanism.
findings.recommendation: Adopt AWS Secrets Manager with automatic rotation. Reference secrets from ECS task definitions via ARNs.

findings.title: No compute hardening or vulnerability scanning
findings.description: Docker image uses node:22-alpine with non-root user but no vulnerability scanning, no hardened base image, no patching strategy.
findings.gap: No patching strategy. Relies on rebuilding with newer base images without scanning for known CVEs.
findings.recommendation: Add container image scanning (ECR scanning or Trivy) to CI/CD. Consider AWS-maintained base images.

findings.title: No security scanning in CI/CD pipeline
findings.description: No Dependabot, SAST, DAST, or container scanning. CI only runs tests and builds. 72+ dependencies unchecked for vulnerabilities.
findings.gap: Vulnerabilities in dependencies and application code reach production undetected.
findings.recommendation: Add Dependabot for dependency scanning. Add CodeQL/Semgrep for SAST. Add ECR image scanning. Configure blocking gates.

findings.title: No distributed tracing
findings.description: No OpenTelemetry, X-Ray, or any tracing instrumentation. Only debug-level logging via the 'debug' library.
findings.gap: Cannot diagnose request flows or identify performance bottlenecks across HTTP → DB → Kafka paths.
findings.recommendation: Instrument with OpenTelemetry SDK for Node.js with X-Ray or ADOT for end-to-end tracing.

findings.title: No SLO definitions
findings.description: No SLOs for event collection availability, dashboard API latency, or data freshness. Only a basic Docker health check exists.
findings.gap: No formal service level objectives. Cannot measure whether the system meets user expectations.
findings.recommendation: Define SLOs: collection endpoint availability ≥99.9%, p99 <200ms; dashboard API p95 <2s.

findings.title: No custom business metrics
findings.description: No CloudWatch metrics, no custom dashboards. The application tracks analytics for users but does not instrument its own operational metrics.
findings.gap: Cannot measure events/sec, active sessions, query latency, or platform health.
findings.recommendation: Publish custom CloudWatch metrics for events collected/sec, sessions/min, error rates by endpoint, query duration.

findings.title: No alerting or anomaly detection
findings.description: No CloudWatch alarms, no PagerDuty integration, no threshold-based alerts. Failures go undetected until user reports.
findings.gap: No alerting — degradations and failures are invisible to operators.
findings.recommendation: Configure CloudWatch alarms for error rates and latency. Enable anomaly detection on the collection endpoint.

findings.title: Direct-to-production deployment with no staged rollout
findings.description: Deployment by pulling latest Docker image. No canary, blue/green, or rolling deployment. No automatic rollback.
findings.gap: Bad releases affect all users immediately with no rollback capability.
findings.recommendation: Adopt ECS blue/green via CodeDeploy or Kubernetes rolling updates with readiness probes on EKS.

findings.title: Integration tests exist but not run in CI
findings.description: 6 Cypress E2E tests with dedicated docker-compose exist. However, CI only runs 3 Jest unit tests. Critical paths (event collection, analytics) untested.
findings.gap: Integration tests not in CI pipeline. Critical workflows have no automated regression protection.
findings.recommendation: Add Cypress tests to CI pipeline. Expand coverage to event collection and analytics queries.

findings.title: No incident response automation
findings.description: No runbooks, no self-healing patterns, no automated remediation. Docker restart policy is the only recovery mechanism.
findings.gap: Incident response is entirely ad hoc. No documented procedures for common failure scenarios.
findings.recommendation: Create runbooks for database failures, Kafka producer issues, and high memory usage. Implement SSM Automation.

findings.title: No observability ownership
findings.description: No CODEOWNERS for monitoring, no service dashboards, no alarms with owners, no SLO team attribution. No observability infrastructure exists.
findings.gap: No observability to own. Monitoring is nonexistent, not just unowned.
findings.recommendation: Establish observability ownership during cloud migration. Define CODEOWNERS for monitoring configuration.

findings.title: No resource tagging governance
findings.description: No AWS resources exist to tag. No tagging standard defined for when infrastructure is created.
findings.gap: No tagging governance. When AWS resources are created, no standard exists for cost allocation or ownership tracking.
findings.recommendation: Define tagging standard (Service, Environment, Team, CostCenter) before creating AWS infrastructure.

top_gaps.title: Infrastructure as Code Coverage
top_gaps.gap: No IaC — all infrastructure manually created via Docker Compose
top_gaps.impact: Cannot reproduce environments, no disaster recovery, no audit trail

top_gaps.title: Managed Compute
top_gaps.gap: Self-hosted Docker containers with no managed container orchestration
top_gaps.impact: No elastic scaling, manual patching, no health-based replacement

top_gaps.title: Managed Databases
top_gaps.impact: No automated failover, manual backups, patching burden

top_gaps.title: Distributed Tracing
top_gaps.impact: Cannot diagnose request flows or identify bottlenecks

top_gaps.title: Application Security Pipeline
top_gaps.gap: No SAST, DAST, or dependency scanning

quick_agent_wins.name: DevOps Agent
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: Agent that triggers deployments, checks build status, and manages Docker image releases
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Add deployment stage to pipeline. Add webhook or API trigger for agent invocation.

quick_agent_wins.name: RAG-based Knowledge Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: Documentation exists in repository (README.md, code comments, 52+ i18n files)
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: Knowledge agent indexing project documentation to assist developers with setup and API usage
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Generate API documentation from route handlers and Zod schemas to enrich the knowledge base.

decomposition_strategy.rationale: APP-Q2=2 indicates identifiable modules with shared database coupling. Clear functional domains exist for incremental extraction.

pathways.name: Move to Cloud Native
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: APP-Q2=2 (primary), INF-Q1=1, APP-Q3=2, APP-Q4=2 (all supporting conditions met)

pathways.name: Move to Containers
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q1=1 but Dockerfile and docker-compose already exist. Application is already containerized — the pathway requires no container definitions to be found.

triggering_questions.note: Score meets threshold but contextual guard blocks: container definitions (Dockerfile, docker-compose) already exist

pathways.not_triggered_reason: Application is already containerized with Dockerfile, docker-compose.yml, and multi-architecture builds. The Move to Containers pathway targets EC2/VM workloads with no containerization.

pathways.name: Move to Open Source
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: DATA-Q4=4 (no stored procedures); PostgreSQL is already open source. No commercial DB engines detected.

triggering_questions.note: Score 4 — no stored procedures or proprietary SQL. Trigger threshold not met.

pathways.not_triggered_reason: PostgreSQL is already open source. No commercial database engines (Oracle, SQL Server) detected. DATA-Q4=4 indicates no proprietary SQL coupling.

pathways.name: Move to Managed Analytics
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q4=2 (self-managed Kafka); data processing workloads exist (ClickHouse analytics ingestion pipeline)

immediate_actions: Replace self-managed Kafka with MSK Serverless or EventBridge
Evaluate Redshift Serverless as ClickHouse replacement

pathways.name: Move to Modern DevOps
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q10=1 (no IaC), OPS-Q5=1 (direct-to-production), OPS-Q6=2 (integration tests not in CI)

immediate_actions: Add deployment stage to GitHub Actions

pathways.name: Move to AI
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context. Context: 'Self-hosted privacy-focused web analytics' contains no AI-related signal terms.
pathways.not_triggered_reason: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context. The context 'Self-hosted privacy-focused web analytics' does not contain AI-related signal terms (AI, agent, LLM, Bedrock, etc.).
